channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Iran’s judiciary vows action against ‘enemy’s mercenaries’ amid executions
item.description: Authorities target accused foreign agents, citing national security, as executions and confiscation of assets intensify.

item.title: Boko Haram attack kills 23 soldiers in Chad’s Lake Chad region
item.description: Chad's army says the attack by the Nigeria-based armed group came on the island of Barka Tolorom.

item.title: LIVE: Arsenal vs Atletico Madrid – Champions League semifinal
item.description: Follow our live build-up with full team news coverage before our text commentary stream of the second leg in London.

item.title: What’s the significance of UAE’s Fujairah, hit on Monday?
item.description: If confirmed, it would be Iran’s first attack on the UAE since the US-Iran ceasefire took effect on April 8.

item.title: What’s happening in Mali one week after attack by armed groups? All we know
item.description: Mali’s military government, Assimi Goita, has taken on the role of defence minister.

item.title: Sudan accuses Ethiopia, UAE of being behind recent drone attacks
item.description: Sudan claims it has evidence that four drone attacks have been launched from Ethiopia with UAE-supplied drones.

item.title: Hegseth says US ‘hasn’t capitulated on anything’ regarding Iran
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told a reporter the US had not capitulated on its demands from Iran.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire holds despite Hormuz standoff: Pentagon chief Hegseth
item.description: Trump aide says the US has established a security 'dome' for ships over Hormuz, but traffic in strait remains stalled.

item.title: Why Europe’s car industry is at the centre of a new US trade war
item.description: Trump has threatened to increase tariffs on EU cars and trucks exported to the US from 15 percent to 25 percent.

item.title: Map of dominance: Why Iran can’t afford to give up Hormuz control
item.description: The vital passageway is key for Tehran as it seeks to leverage in negotiations with Washington.

item.title: Smotrich says he promised his son ‘more destruction’ in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said he promised his son there would be ‘more destruction’ in Lebanon.

item.title: Mapping Mali’s gold and natural resource wealth
item.description: The West African country holds Africa's third-largest gold reserves, plus vast lithium and uranium deposits.

item.title: DRC protest in support of US sanctions against former president
item.description: DRC protest in support of US sanctions against former president

item.title: Hegseth says US blockade on Iran ‘gift to the world’
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the US has gifted the world a ‘powerful red, white, and blue dome’.

item.title: Released Flotilla activists describe‘torture’ in Israeli prison
item.description: Flotilla activists returning home to Argentina say they were subjected to ‘torture’ by Israeli forces who detained them.

item.title: Iran’s football team shows World Cup readiness with social media posts
item.description: Videos from tournament kit reveal photo shoot and images from training sessions highlighting Team Melli's preparations.

item.title: US, Iran, UAE trade Hormuz attack claims: What we know
item.description: Experts say claims and counterclaims threaten the diplomatic route to end the US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Israel extends detention of Gaza flotilla activists, lawyers say
item.description: The lawyers representing the two activists will appeal the extension decision, calling it 'the state's lawlessness'.

item.title: How UK universities ‘spy’ on pro-Palestine students
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Linh Nguyen explains how UK universities paid a private firm to monitor pro-Palestine students.

item.title: CAF is failing Africa’s World Cup fans
item.description: The confederation’s silence on US travel bans and visa restrictions for African fans is shameful.

item.title: Romania PM Ilie Bolojan’s government toppled in no-confidence vote
item.description: Financial markets fear turbulence could mean Romania wavers in its commitment to narrowing budget deficit.

item.title: UK counterterrorism police investigate arson attack at former synagogue
item.description: The incident in London comes after the UK's national threat level was raised to severe last week.

item.title: Has social justice become the new religion of the West?
item.description: Host Mohamed Hassan with guests asks: as religion fades in the West, what replaces it?

item.title: What Modi’s big win in Indian state elections could mean for its democracy
item.description: Modi's BJP won the key state of West Bengal for the first time. But the vote has thrown up questions that won't go away.

item.title: Can central banks curb inflation as energy costs rise?
item.description: Central banks hold rates steady as energy shock tests inflation fight.
